title: Smoky Tangy Mangalorean Fish Curry
description: A fragrant, smoky fish curry packed with tangy tamarind, aromatic herbs, and a hint of char from smoked oil-perfect for kids and paleo diners alike.

ingredients.name: Mixed aromatics (onion, garlic, ginger)
ingredients.notes: finely sliced onion, minced garlic, grated ginger

ingredients.name: Fresh herbs (coriander, mint, curry leaves)
ingredients.amount: 2 tbsp coriander, 1 tbsp mint, 5 curry leaves

instructions: Heat 2 tbsp coconut oil over charcoal until it begins to smoke; remove from heat and set aside.
In a heavy‑bottom pan, heat the smoked oil, add 1 tsp cumin seeds and let them crackle.
Add the mixed aromatics—sliced onion, minced garlic, grated ginger—and sauté until the onion turns translucent.
Stir in 200 ml tomato puree, 1 tsp tamarind paste, 1 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp red chili powder, and a pinch of salt; cook until oil separates.
Gently slide 500 g kingfish fillets into the sauce, turning once to coat.
Pour in 200 ml water, bring to a gentle simmer, cover, and cook for 8–10 minutes until the fish is flaky and cooked through.
Add 2 tbsp chopped coriander, 1 tbsp mint, 5 curry leaves, and 1 tbsp lemon juice; stir gently.
Taste and adjust salt if needed; serve hot with a side of cauliflower rice or a stir‑fry.
